abstract:
Recent advancements in model scaling have enabled large language models (LLMs) to perform a wide array of natural language processing (NLP) tasks in a zero-shot manner, requiring no additional data for task adaptation. The release of ChatGPT, in particular, has captivated the NLP community due to its remarkable ability to generate human-like responses and refine its output through interactive dialogue. However, its potential as a versatile, general-purpose task solver remains largely unexamined.

In this study, we provide a comprehensive empirical evaluation of ChatGPT’s zero-shot learning capabilities across 20 popular NLP datasets spanning seven core task categories. Our findings highlight both the impressive strengths and the inherent limitations of the model. While ChatGPT excels in areas requiring complex reasoning—such as arithmetic tasks—it continues to struggle with specialized tasks like sequence tagging. Alongside our quantitative results, we offer a series of qualitative case studies to provide a deeper understanding of its performance and behavior across different linguistic challenges.
